text.title: Agentic Decision Analysis Workflow
text.summary: Three-pass structured pipeline (frame → score → sensitivity/dissent), parallel scoring agents to reduce single-agent bias, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints.

section.title: Three-Pass Pipeline

rule.statement: Pass 1 (Frame pass, sonnet): reads decision context and emits the structured frame {decision_statement, objectives[], constraints[], decision_maker, criteria[]}. Weights are NOT assigned in this pass. Output JSON only.
rule.rationale: Separating frame from scoring prevents the most common failure: weights assigned by the same agent that generates options, which causes unconscious steering toward a preferred outcome.

rule.statement: Pass 2 (Score pass): for each option × criterion cell, a separate agent retrieves evidence (vendor docs, pricing pages, GitHub metrics) and emits {option, criterion, evidence_urls[], normalized_score_1_5, confidence, caveats[]}. Use multiple agents in parallel — one per criterion or one per option — to reduce single-agent bias. If evidence cannot be found, return confidence=low with empty evidence_urls; never hallucinate a score.
rule.rationale: A single agent scoring the entire matrix inherits its own biases across all cells. Parallel scoring agents with independent evidence retrieval produce more calibrated and disagreement-surfacing results.

rule.statement: Pass 3 (Sensitivity/dissent pass, opus): runs Monte Carlo over weights (±20%) and confidence intervals on cells, emits recommendation with robustness score and lists which cells, if flipped, would change the answer. If gap between #1 and #2 is less than 0.3, recommendation must be "tied — escalate to human".
rule.rationale: The dissent pass is the only pass that can override the apparent matrix winner. Its output is the actual decision recommendation; the matrix is supporting evidence, not the decision.

section.reference: Monte Carlo sensitivity with robustness % and escalation threshold.
LLM prompt for the evidence-backed score pass with evidence URL requirement and banned-source rules.

rule.statement: Three mandatory human-in-the-loop checkpoints: (1) weights before scoring starts, (2) final recommendation before commitment, (3) post-implementation calibration review (compare predicted scores to actual outcomes, 6-12 months later).
rule.rationale: Agents produce the matrix; humans own the decision and the calibration review. Without the calibration review, the team never learns whether its scoring rubrics were accurate, so quality never improves.

rule.statement: Archive the final matrix and rationale in .aidocs/ alongside the project's other artifacts. Future teams must be able to audit the decision and improve calibration from historical data.
rule.rationale: Decision quality improves when previous decisions are reviewed against outcomes. An unarchived decision cannot be audited, calibrated, or defended in a post-mortem.
